mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and economic activity, which predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days due to improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when INDPRO increases (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the unemployment rate below a key threshold signals accelerating labor market recovery, boosting consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In all triggered instances where UNRATE falls below 6.5%, the subsequent 5-day SPX return is negative in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains at the zero lower bound while inflation rises, real interest rates turn deeply negative, pushing investors into equities and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, the 20-day SPX return is negative more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in real GDP after a severe contraction signals the start of a durable economic expansion, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading quarters (one quarter).
failure_condition: If quarterly GDPC1 rises and the 60-day forward SPX return is negative in more than 60% of such quarters over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1.4 million units indicate robust residential investment and consumer optimism, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as the real estate sector benefits.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1400, the 20-day SPX return is negative in more than 40% of instances, or the average return is not significantly greater than the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous improvement in both payroll employment (above 140 million) and decline in unemployment (below 8%) provides a strong signal of labor market healing, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the 20-day SPX return is negative in more than 50% of cases over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales reflect strong consumer spending, which drives corporate revenues and earnings, thereby pushing S&P 500 prices higher over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS trend and 20-day forward SPX return is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-rate regime, a rise in consumer prices above a threshold forces expectations of future monetary tightening, which weighs on equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL exceeds 261, the 20-day SPX return is positive in more than 50% of instances, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).
